name: Poutines Rapees
recipeInstructions:
For many Acadians living in southeastern New Brunswick, poultine rapee – a potato dumpling dish with seasoned pork in the center – is considered a national dish. In other parts of Acadia, these delicacies are prepared without meat and sometimes fish is added to fricot. Although the greyish color and gluey texture make them unappetizing, their taste compensates for appearance.
1. Soak the pork overnight in cold water to remove excess salt, then cut into cubes.
2. Squeeze out water from grated potatoes using a cotton bag.
3. Mix mashed potatoes with grated potatoes.
4. Season mixture with salt and pepper.
5. Roll mixture into balls resembling small snowballs; make a hole in each center and add about 1 tablespoon of pork.
6. Dust the poultines with flour.
7. Gently drop 2-3 poultines at a time into a large pot of boiling, salted water, maintaining a rolling boil.
8. Simmer for 2-3 hours.
9. Serve hot with butter, salt, and pepper, or as dessert with sugar and molasses.
MAKES: 6 POULTINES
SOURCE: A Taste of Acadie by Marielle Cormier-Boudrau
